title: US earnings season - Q1 2026
author: Sigma Capital

excerpt: US earnings season for Q1 2026 is forecasted to show a 13.2% growth for the S&P 500, marking the sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit earnings growth. The Information Technology sector is driving this growth with a 45% expansion. Earnings for the Finance sector are expected to increase by 26.7%

content.introduction: US earnings season for Q1 2026 is forecasted to show a 13.2% growth for the S&P 500, marking the sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit earnings growth. The Information Technology sector is driving this growth with a 45% expansion. Earnings for the Finance sector are expected to increase by 26.7%.

Total Q1 earnings for the Zacks Investment Banks/Managers industry, of which JPMorgan, Citigroup (C Quick QuoteC  -  Free Report) , and Wells Fargo are a part, are expected to increase by +19.7% from the same period last year on +10.5% higher revenues, as the table below shows. For the Finance sector as a whole, Q1 earnings are expected to increase by +26.7% on +9.8% higher revenues, which will follow the sector’s +17.3% earnings growth on +7.4% higher revenues in the preceding period.

### **Join us April 29:** Leveraging Data and Earnings Insights in a Volatile 2026 Market****. The S&P 500® is projected to deliver its sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit earnings growth at 13.2%, fueled largely by a powerhouse 45% expansion in the Information Technology sector. The Q1 2026 earnings season has officially started, and the early results suggest a market that is largely defying the geopolitical fog we’ve discussed. TSMC posted record Q1 revenue on the back of surging 2nm and 3nm chip demand7, while ASML raised its full-year 2026 sales guidance to as high as €40 billion, citing an "AI Supercycle" that is outpacing current tool supply.8 These results suggest that chip names are being fueled by a structural infrastructure race rather than cyclical hype, as memory makers like Samsung and SK Hynix accelerate capacity expansions to keep up. Including reports from 28 S&P 500 names last week, the blended EPS growth rate increased to 13.2% from 12.6% last week, the sixth quarter of double digits bottom-line growth.

For Q1 2026, geopolitical [risk](https://www.ig.com/en/glossary-trading-terms/risks-definition) has taken its place. Eight of the eleven [sectors](https://www.ig.com/en/glossary-trading-terms/sectors-definition) are projected to report YoY earnings growth in Q1 2026, led by Information Technology (+45.0%), Materials (+24.2%), and Financials (+15.1%). ## [Markets](https://www.ig.com/en/markets-to-trade).
